front: Work: Azaro is an abiku (spirit child) who observes his boxer father nicknamed 'Black Tyger' in this novel
Work: Madame Koto's bar is frequented by spirits; she joins the Party of the Rich and keeps the protagonist as a good luck charm
Work: the Party of the Rich opposes the Party of the Poor; Azaro's father fights a man from the Party of the Rich
Work: this novel opens with 'In the beginning there was a river'
Work: ghosts eat dinner at the house of the police officer responsible for their deaths in this novel's opening chapter
Work: villagers are temporarily blinded by yellow butterflies raining from the sky in this novel
Work: Jeremiah the photographer is repeatedly arrested for producing photographs in this novel
Work: 'Anyone who thinks evil of you, may this car run them over in their sleep' is a blessing spoken over a new automobile
Work: a 'beautiful beggar girl with a bad eye' accompanies the narrator's father to a betting shop
Author: in this author's The Freedom Artist, painting 'Who is the prisoner?' on a wall leads to the disappearance of Amalantis, sought by her lover Karnak
Author: this author's debut novel Flowers and Shadows follows Jeffia Okwe witnessing his corrupt father's downfall
Author: this author adapted Camus's The Stranger into a 2018 play with a short film from 'the Arab's' perspective
Author: in this author's short story 'In the City of Red Dust,' a governor scrambles fighter planes for his birthday while inhabitants starve
Author: this author won the Bad Sex in Fiction Award for The Age of Magic
Author: the only Nigerian winner of the Booker Prize
